edges.subject: Nemotron 3 Super 120B-A12B chat
edges.description: Per the lattice's description for the GenRM, nvidia/Qwen3-Nemotron-235B-A22B-GenRM-2603 was used as the Generative Reward Model (judge) in RLHF training of NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B, scoring response pairs to produce the helpfulness/ranking labels backing Nemotron-RLHF-GenRM-v1 (77% of the rlhf split). The GenRM is built on Qwen/Qwen3-235B-A22B-Thinking-2507 with 128K context.

evidence.excerpt: It was used in RLHF training of NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B and accepts inputs up to 128K tokens.
evidence.explanation: Audit-artifact lattice description explicitly identifies this GenRM as the judge used during the Super model's RLHF training stage.

evidence.excerpt: This dataset is designed to train Generative Reward Models (GenRMs).
evidence.explanation: Confirms that Nemotron-RLHF-GenRM-v1 supplies GenRM-judge preference data — consistent with using a GenRM model as the filter for the rlhf split.

edges.subject: Nemotron 3 Super 120B-A12B chat

evidence.explanation: Names DeepSeek-R1-0528 as a generator of expert trajectories used as ground-truth tool-call choices.

evidence.explanation: Card lists the four expert generators (Data Collection) and two labelers (Labeling Method) used to build the conversational tool-use trajectories.

evidence.excerpt: [DeepSeek-R1-0528](https://huggingface.co/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-0528) ... We then generate solutions to each new coding problem using a reasoning model.

References cite DeepSeek-R1-0528 as a key dependency for the v2 dataset's solution generation.

evidence.excerpt: DeepSeek-R1-0528 produces multiple candidate outputs per prompt (8 responses for SWE-Bench-Train / SWE-reBench / SWE-Smith and 4 responses for SWE-Fixer-Train).
evidence.explanation: Card explicitly cites DeepSeek-R1-0528 as the agentless candidate generator.

edges.subject: nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Nano-30B-A3B-BF16
edges.description: BF16 chat model continues from Base-BF16 checkpoint into the multi-stage post-training pipeline (SFT then synchronous-GRPO RL with RLHF refinement).

evidence.explanation: Base-BF16 is pre-trained in Stage 1; Stage 2 'further fine-tuned' implies the chat continues weights from Base.

evidence.excerpt: [NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Nano-30B-A3B-Base-BF16](https://huggingface.co/nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Nano-30B-A3B-Base-BF16) model was pre-trained using crawled and synthetic code, math, science, and general knowledge data.

Recipe wires the pretrained Nano3 Base checkpoint as the SFT init.

evidence.excerpt: SFT is implemented using Megatron-Bridge's finetune() entry point, which loads a pretrained checkpoint and handles the training loop with role-based loss masking.
evidence.explanation: Confirms SFT continues from the pretrained Base checkpoint.

evidence.excerpt: We post-trained Nemotron 3 Nano using three approaches: supervised fine tuning (SFT), multi-environment reinforcement learning from verifiable rewards (RLVR), and reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF).
evidence.explanation: States the post-trained checkpoint continues from the base via the three post-training stages.
SFT config explicitly sets the base pretrain artifact as the warm-start.

evidence.excerpt: We post-trained Nemotron 3 Nano using three approaches: supervised fine tuning (SFT), multi-environment reinforcement learning from verifiable rewards (RLVR), and reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF).
evidence.explanation: Indicates the post-trained model is the base model after the post-training pipeline.

evidence.excerpt: NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Nano-30B-A3B-Base-BF16 model was pre-trained using crawled and synthetic code, math, science, and general knowledge data.
evidence.explanation: The Base-BF16 checkpoint is explicitly the artifact produced by Stage 1, which Stage 2 SFT and Stage 3 RL build on.

evidence.excerpt: We post-trained Nemotron 3 Nano using three approaches: supervised fine tuning (SFT), multi-environment reinforcement learning from verifiable rewards (RLVR), and reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF).
evidence.explanation: Final BF16 inherits weights from the Base checkpoint.

edges.subject: nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Nano-30B-A3B-FP8
edges.description: FP8 model is the post-training-quantized version of the BF16 chat checkpoint, with attention and feeding-Mamba layers retained in BF16 to preserve accuracy.

evidence.explanation: Card frontmatter explicitly identifies BF16 as the base and quantized as the relation.

evidence.excerpt: The model including the KV cache was quantized into FP8.
evidence.explanation: Direct PTQ derivation of FP8 from BF16.

evidence.excerpt: After post-training the model in BF16, we applied Post-Training Quantization (PTQ) using ModelOpt and Megatron-LM to quantize the model to FP8.
evidence.explanation: Establishes the BF16→FP8 derivation of the FP8 release from the BF16 post-trained checkpoint.
FP8 release is the quantized derivative of the BF16 instruct release.

evidence.excerpt: After post-training the model in BF16, we applied Post-Training Quantization (PTQ) using ModelOpt and Megatron-LM to quantize the model to FP8.
evidence.explanation: Documents the BF16 -> FP8 derivation.

evidence.excerpt: For PTQ calibration, we used a small subset containing 1K samples from post-training reasoning SFT dataset.
evidence.explanation: Documents derivation of the FP8 model from the BF16 post-trained checkpoint.

evidence.excerpt: After post-training the model in BF16, we applied Post-Training Quantization (PTQ) using ModelOpt and Megatron-LM to quantize the model to FP8.
evidence.explanation: FP8 starts from BF16 post-trained checkpoint.
YAML directly states FP8 derives from BF16 via quantization.

evidence.excerpt: The model including the KV cache was quantized into FP8. To preserve accuracy while improving efficiency, we applied a selective quantization strategy, keeping the attention layers and the Mamba layers that feed into those attention layers in BF16.
evidence.explanation: Explicitly identifies Stage 4 as post-training FP8 quantization (no further training).

evidence.excerpt: After post-training the model in BF16, we applied Post-Training Quantization (PTQ) using ModelOpt and Megatron-LM to quantize the model to FP8.
evidence.explanation: FP8 inherits weights from BF16.

evidence.excerpt: After post-training the model in BF16, we applied Post-Training Quantization (PTQ) using ModelOpt and Megatron-LM to quantize the model to FP8.
evidence.explanation: States BF16 → FP8 PTQ derivation.

evidence.excerpt: We performed quantization sensitivity analysis ... we keep them in BF16. ... The model weights, activations, and KV cache are quantized to FP8.
evidence.explanation: Describes the selective FP8 quantization recipe applied to the BF16 post-trained checkpoint.

edges.subject: nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Nano-30B-A3B-NVFP4
edges.description: NVFP4 model initialized from BF16 chat checkpoint, then quantized via PTQ followed by Quantization-Aware Distillation against the BF16 teacher.

evidence.explanation: Frontmatter declares BF16 as the base of the quantized NVFP4 model.

evidence.excerpt: The model was quantized to NVFP4 with KV Cache quantized to FP8 using Post-Training Quantization (PTQ).
evidence.explanation: Direct PTQ + QAD derivation of NVFP4 from BF16.
YAML directly states NVFP4 derives from BF16 via quantization.

evidence.excerpt: The model was quantized to NVFP4 with KV Cache quantized to FP8 using Post-Training Quantization (PTQ). ... After PTQ, Quantization-Aware Distillation (QAD) was applied for further accuracy recovery.
evidence.explanation: Confirms NVFP4 is derived from BF16 via PTQ plus QAD.
Both BF16 and NVFP4 belong to the same v3 collection; the NVFP4 entry is the quantized counterpart of the post-trained BF16 release.

edges.subject: nvidia/Nemotron-3-Nano-Omni-30B-A3B-Reasoning-FP8
edges.description: FP8 variant is produced by quantizing the BF16 release: per-tensor E4M3 on every LM linear layer except MoE router and lm_head, FP8 KV cache, and BF16-preserved vision/audio encoders and their MLP projectors. Yields 8.5 effective bits/weight, 32.8 GB.

evidence.excerpt: We release FP8 and NVFP4 quantized variants alongside the BF16 model. The FP8 variant quantizes every linear layer in the language model to per-tensor E4M3 (with the exception of the MoE router and lm_head) and pairs it with an FP8 KV cache, yielding 8.5 effective bits per weight (32.8 GB).
evidence.explanation: Explicitly states the FP8 variant is derived by quantizing the BF16 release.

evidence.excerpt: The FP8 variant quantizes every linear layer in the language model to per-tensor E4M3 (with the exception of the MoE router and lm_head) and pairs it with an FP8 KV cache, yielding 8.5 effective bits per weight (32.8 GB).
evidence.explanation: Confirms the FP8 release is a quantized derivative of the BF16 weights.

evidence.excerpt: The FP8 variant quantizes every linear layer in the language model to per-tensor E4M3 (with the exception of the MoE router and `lm_head`) and pairs it with an FP8 KV cache
evidence.explanation: Establishes FP8 as a quantization-derived variant of BF16.

evidence.excerpt: The FP8 variant quantizes every linear layer in the language model to per-tensor E4M3 ... yielding 8.5 effective bits per weight (32.8 GB).
evidence.explanation: Confirms FP8 weights are derived from the BF16 release via PTQ.

edges.subject: nvidia/Nemotron-3-Nano-Omni-30B-A3B-Reasoning-NVFP4
edges.description: NVFP4 variant is produced by quantizing the BF16 release with a mixed-precision recipe (NVFP4 routed MoE experts, FP8 Mamba in_proj/out_proj/shared experts/attention o_proj, BF16 vision/audio encoders + MLP projectors). Yields 4.98 effective bits/weight, 20.9 GB.

evidence.explanation: Frontmatter declares BF16 as the base model from which NVFP4 is derived.

evidence.excerpt: The NVFP4 variant uses a mixed-precision recipe inspired by Nemotron 3 Super: routed MoE experts are quantized to NVFP4 ... yielding 4.98 effective bits per weight (20.9 GB). In both variants the vision and audio encoders and their MLP projectors are kept in BF16.
evidence.explanation: Specifies the NVFP4 quantization recipe applied to the BF16 release.
HF model-card frontmatter explicitly names the BF16 release as the base model the NVFP4 variant was quantized from.
NVFP4 model card frontmatter explicitly names BF16 as its base.
Frontmatter explicitly declares the NVFP4 release as derived from the BF16 base.

edges.subject: nvidia/Nemotron-3-Nano-Omni-30B-A3B-Reasoning-NVFP4
edges.description: NVFP4 quantization recipe is inspired by the Nemotron 3 Super NVFP4 release: same mixed-precision treatment of MoE experts vs. Mamba/attention/shared-expert paths, vision/audio encoders kept in BF16. No weight inheritance, only methodology.

evidence.excerpt: The NVFP4 variant uses a mixed-precision recipe inspired by Nemotron 3 Super
evidence.explanation: Explicitly attributes the NVFP4 recipe to Nemotron 3 Super.

evidence.excerpt: Each problem is solved multiple times by the gpt-oss-120b model under six settings (high/medium/low reasoning x with/without Python TIR).

evidence.explanation: Confirms OpenCodeReasoning as the upstream problem source for InfiniByte.

edges.subject: nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B-BF16
edges.description: DeepSeek-V3.2 (and the Speciale variant, which is not in the lattice and is mapped to DeepSeek-V3.2) generated the reasoning trajectories in Nemotron-Math-v3: Speciale produced CoT solutions while DeepSeek-V3.2 produced Python TIR solutions. These traces train the Nemotron-3-Super-120B BF16 chat through SFT.

evidence.excerpt: Chain-of-thought (CoT) solutions are generated using DeepSeek-V3.2-Speciale, while Python Tool-Integrated Reasoning (TIR) solutions are generated using DeepSeek-V3.2.
evidence.explanation: Establishes both DeepSeek-V3.2 / DeepSeek-V3.2-Speciale as the trace generators.

evidence.excerpt: The tool-calling subset uses DeepSeek-V3.2 and GLM-4.6 for simulation and judging.
evidence.explanation: Names DeepSeek-V3.2 as a simulator (and judge) in tool_calling.

evidence.explanation: Names DeepSeek-V3.2 as a refresh teacher.

evidence.excerpt: we use DeepSeek-V3.2 (DeepSeek-AI, 2025a) as the primary engine to generate step-by-step solution trajectories
evidence.explanation: Documents DeepSeek-V3.2 as the terminal trajectory generator.

evidence.excerpt: We scale this pipeline with DeepSeek-v3.2 (DeepSeek-AI et al., 2025) and GLM-4.7

edges.subject: nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B-FP8
edges.description: FP8 release is produced via post-training quantization from the BF16 chat model: per the Hopper GPU target, MoE/Mamba GEMMs and KV cache go to FP8; Mamba SSM cache in FP16; embedding/attention/output layers stay in BF16. Calibrated with 256 SFT samples at 65536 context.

evidence.excerpt: We apply post-training quantization (PTQ) using Model-Optimizer to quantize weights and activations to generate two efficient deployment checkpoints: FP8 (W8A8) for Hopper and NVFP4 (W4A4) for Blackwell.
evidence.explanation: States both quantized variants are derived from the BF16 chat checkpoint.

evidence.excerpt: Both checkpoints are produced using Model Optimizer PTQ with Megatron-Bridge ... Calibration used 256 samples from the post-training SFT dataset.
evidence.explanation: Documents the BF16 -> FP8 PTQ derivation.

evidence.excerpt: We apply post-training quantization (PTQ) using Model-Optimizer to quantize weights and activations to generate two efficient deployment checkpoints: FP8 (W8A8) for Hopper and NVFP4 (W4A4) for Blackwell.
evidence.explanation: States that FP8 is a quantization of the BF16 checkpoint.

evidence.excerpt: FP8 (W8A8) | Hopper (H100) | FP8 weights and activations
evidence.explanation: Identifies FP8 as a quantization derivative of the post-trained release.

evidence.excerpt: Both checkpoints are produced using [Model Optimizer](.../examples/llm_ptq) PTQ
evidence.explanation: FP8 produced via PTQ from the BF16 release.

evidence.excerpt: The FP8 checkpoint quantizes MoE GEMMs and Mamba GEMMs to FP8, with FP8 KV Cache quantization. ... Calibration used 256 samples from the post-training SFT dataset.
evidence.explanation: Confirms the FP8 checkpoint is derived from the post-trained BF16 model via PTQ calibration.

evidence.excerpt: Both checkpoints are produced using Model Optimizer PTQ with Megatron-Bridge.
evidence.explanation: Confirms PTQ is the production process from the BF16 checkpoint.

evidence.excerpt: We apply post-training quantization (PTQ) using Model-Optimizer to quantize weights and activations to generate two efficient deployment checkpoints: FP8 (W8A8) for Hopper
evidence.explanation: FP8 checkpoint is derived from the BF16 chat model via PTQ.

evidence.excerpt: FP8 Checkpoint | BF16 Baseline ... MoE GEMM (Sparse Experts and Shared Experts) | FP8 | BF16

evidence.excerpt: we apply post-training quantization (PTQ) using Model-Optimizer to quantize weights and activations to generate two efficient deployment checkpoints: FP8 (W8A8) for Hopper and NVFP4 (W4A4) for Blackwell.
evidence.explanation: Confirms FP8 quantizes the post-trained BF16 Instruct checkpoint.

evidence.excerpt: We apply post-training quantization (PTQ) using Model-Optimizer to quantize weights and activations to generate two efficient deployment checkpoints: FP8 (W8A8) for Hopper and NVFP4 (W4A4) for Blackwell.
evidence.explanation: FP8 inherits weights from BF16.

evidence.excerpt: Two quantized checkpoints are released: FP8 (W8A8) ... BF16 Baseline

edges.subject: nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B-NVFP4
edges.description: NVFP4 release is produced via post-training quantization from the BF16 chat model with mixed-precision recipe (NVFP4 sparse-expert GEMMs, FP8/BF16 attention/Mamba, BF16 embeddings/output) selected by Model-Optimizer AutoQuantize. Calibration: 512 samples at SL=4096 from Super SFT dataset. Achieved 99.8% median accuracy relative to BF16.

evidence.excerpt: We apply post-training quantization (PTQ) using Model-Optimizer to quantize weights and activations to generate two efficient deployment checkpoints: FP8 (W8A8) for Hopper and NVFP4 (W4A4) for Blackwell.
evidence.explanation: Confirms both quantized variants derive from the same BF16 chat checkpoint.

evidence.excerpt: QAD uses the BF16 checkpoint as teacher and the NVFP4 checkpoint as student
evidence.explanation: Confirms BF16 is the parent checkpoint and teacher for the NVFP4 release.

evidence.excerpt: QAD uses the BF16 checkpoint as teacher and the NVFP4 checkpoint as student
evidence.explanation: Confirms BF16 as teacher and NVFP4 as student.

evidence.excerpt: Precision settings for the backbone NVFP4 checkpoint compared with the BF16 baseline.
evidence.explanation: States NVFP4 is derived from the BF16 baseline.

evidence.excerpt: NVFP4 (W4A4) | Blackwell (B200) | NVFP4 weights and activations | 1.5--2.2x higher GEMM FLOPS than FP8
evidence.explanation: Identifies NVFP4 as a quantization derivative of the post-trained release.
Names NVFP4 as a quantized variant of the post-trained release.

evidence.excerpt: QAD uses the BF16 checkpoint as teacher and the NVFP4 checkpoint as student
evidence.explanation: BF16 is the QAD teacher producing the NVFP4 student release.

evidence.excerpt: NVFP4 (W4A4) for Blackwell. ... The mixed-precision PTQ process completed in less than 2 hours ... using 512 samples from the Nemotron 3 Super SFT dataset
evidence.explanation: NVFP4 checkpoint is derived from the BF16 chat model via mixed-precision PTQ.

evidence.excerpt: QAD uses the BF16 checkpoint as teacher and the NVFP4 checkpoint as student
evidence.explanation: NVFP4 student distilled from BF16 teacher.

evidence.excerpt: For running Nemotron 3 Super on a single B200 or DGX Spark - please see: [NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B-NVFP4]
evidence.explanation: The BF16 card points to the NVFP4 release as a quantized version for single-GPU deployment, derived from the BF16 release. Supports the trained_from edge from nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B-NVFP4 to nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B-BF16.

evidence.excerpt: The resulting model achieved 99.8% median accuracy relative to the BF16 baseline while retaining near-FP4 performance.
evidence.explanation: Confirms NVFP4 derives from BF16 Instruct via PTQ.

evidence.excerpt: Combining AutoQuantize with the improved NVFP4 PTQ recipe produced a mostly-FP4 model, with only a small subset of layers retained in FP8 or BF16 for accuracy preservation.
evidence.explanation: NVFP4 inherits weights from BF16.

evidence.excerpt: QAD uses the BF16 checkpoint as teacher and the NVFP4 checkpoint as student
evidence.explanation: BF16 checkpoint initialises NVFP4 and supervises QAD.

edges.subject: nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B-BF16
edges.description: SPEED-Bench (off-lattice; Abramovich et al. 2026, https://research.nvidia.com/publication/2026-02_speed-bench-unified-and-diverse-benchmark-speculative-decoding) used as the speculative-decoding evaluation benchmark for the Super MTP heads.

evidence.excerpt: We evaluate MTP quality using SPEED-Bench (Abramovich et al., 2026), a benchmark tailored for speculative decoding.

edges.subject: nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-Nano-9B-v2
edges.description: nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-Nano-9B-v2 was pretrained on Nemotron-Pretraining-SFT-v1, a diverse synthetically generated and curated SFT-style dataset (~316.5B tokens) spanning STEM, multilingual, academic and reasoning domains; STEM expanded from math/science seeds via multi-iteration generation with Qwen3 and DeepSeek; academic QA from undergraduate/graduate texts; billions of tokens with DeepSeek-V3 and Qwen3.

edges.subject: Nemotron
edges.description: NVIDIA's MultiTurnChat-v1 dataset adopts the published MultiChallenge benchmark methodology (ScaleAI, arxiv 2501.17399) — multi-turn 'model-breaking' framing, focus on inference memory / instruction retention / version editing / self-coherence — and re-implements it within NeMo Gym for Nemotron training.

edges.subject: nvidia/Nemotron-3-Nano-Omni-30B-A3B-Reasoning-BF16
edges.description: Question generation for RQA was seeded with random ~4096-character chunks drawn from the STEM subset of Essential-Web (EssentialAI/eai-taxonomy-stem-w-dclm) under a hierarchical round-robin sampling over Free-Decimal-Correspondence subdomains; the seed text was used as inspiration only and not allowed to appear in the final RQA samples.

evidence.excerpt: Starting from the STEM subset of Essential-Web... The text serves only as inspiration for the question. You *must not* reference the text directly in any way.
evidence.explanation: Names the EssentialAI STEM subset as the seed corpus and confirms its inspiration-only role in generation.

edges.subject: nvidia/Nemotron-H-8B-Base-8K
edges.description: An internal Nemotron-H 8B checkpoint was used for the Phase-2 continued pretraining ablation that validated RQA: 18k extra steps starting from a 140k-step checkpoint with either 4% (4.5M samples) or 8% (9M samples) of the data blend replaced by RQA, taking weight from the high-quality Common Crawl bucket.

evidence.excerpt: we ran continued pretraining experiments on an internal checkpoint of Nemotron-H 8B... comparing the result of replacing either 4% or 8% of the existing data blend with RQA samples
evidence.explanation: Documents Nemotron-H 8B as the design-space ablation vehicle used to measure RQA's training effect.

edges.subject: nvidia/Nemotron-H-8B-Base-8K
edges.description: The Nemotron-H 8B continued-pretraining ablations for RQA were scored on MMLU-Pro (with chain-of-thought) — the 8% RQA mix lifted MMLU-Pro from 43.39 to 45.80.

evidence.explanation: Table column names MMLU-Pro as one of the eval benchmarks used for the RQA ablation.

edges.subject: nvidia/Nemotron-H-8B-Base-8K
edges.description: Math 500 (with chain-of-thought) was one of the held-out reasoning evals tracked across the RQA ablations on Nemotron-H 8B, lifting from 71.00 to 73.40 with RQA in the data blend.

evidence.explanation: Table column shows Math 500 as a reasoning eval for the Nemotron-H 8B RQA ablations.

edges.subject: nvidia/Nemotron-H-8B-Base-8K
edges.description: GSM8K (with chain-of-thought) was tracked across Nemotron-H 8B RQA ablations as a math-reasoning probe; the 8% RQA mix raised GSM8K from 81.96 to 84.76.

evidence.explanation: Table column lists GSM8K among the RQA evaluation benchmarks.

edges.subject: nvidia/Nemotron-H-8B-Base-8K
edges.description: HumanEval+ was tracked alongside the math/science benchmarks to verify that RQA's reasoning data also improved coding ability on Nemotron-H 8B (38.14 baseline → 47.20 with 4% RQA).

evidence.explanation: Table column names HumanEval+ as a coding benchmark in the RQA ablation.

edges.subject: nvidia/Nemotron-H-8B-Base-8K
edges.description: MBPP+ was the second coding benchmark used to score the RQA ablations on Nemotron-H 8B (48.68 → 54.84 with 4% RQA).

evidence.explanation: Table column names MBPP+ as a coding benchmark in the RQA ablation.

edges.subject: nvidia/Nemotron-3-Nano-Omni-30B-A3B-Reasoning-BF16
edges.description: Nemotron Nano v3 (lattice concept formal_name 'Nemotron 3 Nano') was trained on the 9,949-sample structured-outputs corpus that Data Designer produced; the dataset is designed for RLVR with schema-conformance reward but was also reported to improve the model in SFT/RLVR mixes.

evidence.excerpt: Training Nemotron Nano v3 with this data improved JSONSchemaBench accuracy from 80.2% to 86.9%... The dataset is publicly available on HuggingFace: nvidia/Nemotron-RL-instruction_following-structured_outputs
evidence.explanation: Documents Nemotron Nano v3 as the consumer of the published nvidia/Nemotron-RL-instruction_following-structured_outputs dataset.

edges.subject: nvidia/Nemotron-3-Nano-Omni-30B-A3B-Reasoning-BF16
edges.description: Every column of the structured-outputs pipeline (schema generation, conversation/document, three rollouts of structured JSON/YAML/XML output) was synthesised by Qwen/Qwen3-235B-A22B-Instruct-2507 — explicitly named as the generator model on the published dataset card and used as alias 'Qwen3-235B' in the pipeline.

evidence.excerpt: Generator model | Qwen3-235B-A22B-Instruct-2507... STAGE 3: LLM GENERATION (Qwen3-235B)
evidence.explanation: Pipeline diagram and dataset card both name Qwen3-235B-A22B-Instruct-2507 as the synthesiser for the structured-outputs corpus that trained Nemotron Nano v3.

edges.subject: Nemotron 3 Super
edges.description: BIRD's 1,534-sample dev split was used (5-run average) to measure the impact of the synthetic text-to-SQL dataset on Nemotron Super v3 SFT — the headline result is a +15-point lift in execution accuracy from 26.77% to 41.80%.

evidence.excerpt: On the BIRD SQL benchmark (1,534 dev samples, 5-run average), Nemotron Super achieves 41.80% EX... raised Nemotron Super's EX on BIRD by 15 points, from 26.77% to 41.80%.
evidence.explanation: Documents BIRD as the eval target for Nemotron Super v3's text-to-SQL training.

edges.subject: Nemotron 3 Super
edges.description: Qwen/Qwen3-235B-A22B (alias 'riddle-gen') generated both the draft search riddle from each Wikidata graph path and the BrowseComp-style obfuscated rewrite — these riddles became the user prompts for the search-agent SFT dataset that trained Nemotron Super v3.

evidence.explanation: Pipeline code names qwen/qwen3-235b-a22b as the riddle/question generator for the search-agent SFT corpus consumed by Nemotron Super v3.
Table cell pins Qwen3-235B-A22B as a generator for Unconditional-Algorithmic.
Table cell pins Qwen3-235B-A22B as a generator for the Multiple-Choice split.
Per-subset table credits Qwen3-235B-A22B as a generator for Unconditional-Algorithmic and Multiple-Choice.

edges.subject: Nemotron 3 Super
edges.description: MiniMaxAI/MiniMax-M2 was the rollout model that solved each obfuscated riddle by emitting multi-step search/read/reason trajectories against Tavily — ~12 tool calls per sample, 6,974 'stop' completions — and those trajectories became the SFT trace records that trained Nemotron Super v3.

evidence.excerpt: Rollout model: MiniMax-M2 (strong BrowseComp + tool-calling scores)... The rollout model was MiniMax-M2, chosen for its strong performance on BrowseComp and tool-calling benchmarks.
evidence.explanation: Pipeline diagram and prose name MiniMax-M2 as the rollout model whose trajectories trained Nemotron Super v3.

edges.subject: Nemotron 3 Super
edges.description: The seed corpus for the search-agent dataset was generated by random walks on the Wikidata knowledge graph (50,000 multi-hop seed paths with anti-meta filters and 4-8 hop bounds), making Wikidata the structural source of every riddle that Nemotron Super v3 trained on.

evidence.excerpt: We used Wikidata SPARQL queries to fetch neighbors at each hop... 50,000 JSONL records, each containing hops[], seed_entity, final_answer_entity, readable_path, and path_length.
evidence.explanation: Documents the Wikidata KG as the seed-path source whose multi-hop structure shaped the Nemotron-Super search-agent training data.

edges.subject: Nemotron 3 Super
edges.description: BrowseComp (1,266 web browsing problems) is the headline eval used to measure the synthetic search-agent dataset's impact on Nemotron Super v3 — accuracy moved from 0% to 31.28% after SFT+RL with the search-agent corpus.

evidence.excerpt: On the BrowseComp benchmark (1,266 web browsing problems), Nemotron Super went from 0% to 31.28% accuracy... Nemotron Super (after synthetic search agent data, SFT + RL) | 31.28
evidence.explanation: Names BrowseComp as the eval benchmark for the search-agent training event.

edges.subject: nvidia/Nemotron-3-Nano-Omni-30B-A3B-Reasoning-BF16
edges.description: Phase 1 of the VLM SDG pipeline used Nemotron-Parse-v1.1 as the OCR model to transcribe PDF page images (Common Crawl + SEC + arXiv corpora) into structured text with bounding boxes and semantic classes; the OCR output then seeded text-QA generation that fed into Nemotron-3-Nano-Omni-30B-A3B's training blend.

evidence.excerpt: The first pipeline split the problem into OCR transcription with Nemotron Parse and question-answer generation from those transcripts with GPT-OSS-120B... model_alias='ocr',  # Nemotron-Parse-v1.1
evidence.explanation: Names Nemotron-Parse-v1.1 as the OCR model that transformed PDF pages into the transcript content used to train the Omni model.

edges.subject: nvidia/Nemotron-3-Nano-Omni-30B-A3B-Reasoning-BF16
edges.description: Phase 2 introduced Qwen3-VL-30B-A3B as the page-classification model: it tagged 8.2M Common Crawl pages with 8 primary visual-element categories and 45 subcategories plus a contains_reasoning_content boolean, which dropped 72% of pages before the expensive QA stage and decided which pages contributed to the Omni training data.

evidence.excerpt: model_alias='qwen-vl',  # Qwen3-VL-30B-A3B... 72% of pages were plain text, decorative images, or content without reasoning potential. This filtering step was critical: the expensive QA generation stage only ran on the pages where visual reasoning questions were actually possible.
evidence.explanation: Pipeline code names Qwen3-VL-30B-A3B as the classifier model whose decisions filtered which pages reached QA generation.

edges.subject: nvidia/Nemotron-3-Nano-Omni-30B-A3B-Reasoning-BF16
edges.description: After SFT the team ran GRPO on the best checkpoint; single-page rollouts came mostly from MMPR (open vision-preference dataset by OpenGVLab) which provides clean numeric / short-list / multiple-choice answers that score against ground truth — those rollouts entered the RL training mix that produced the +3.3-point post-RL gain on MMLongBench-Doc.

evidence.excerpt: The single-page rollouts came mostly from MMPR (https://huggingface.co/datasets/OpenGVLab/MMPR), where the answers are numbers, short lists, or multiple-choice and score cleanly against ground truth
evidence.explanation: Names OpenGVLab/MMPR as the source of single-page RL rollouts whose ground-truth-scored trajectories trained the Omni model.

edges.subject: OpenResearcher/OpenResearcher-30B-A3B
edges.description: OpenResearcher's 30B-A3B model was built by fine-tuning the published Nemotron Nano 3 (NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Nano-30B-A3B-BF16) checkpoint on synthetic deep-research trajectories — the dev note explicitly links the trained model to the Nemotron Nano 3 HF checkpoint as its starting weights.

evidence.excerpt: synthetic trajectories... are sufficient to train Nemotron Nano 3 (https://huggingface.co/nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Nano-30B-A3B-BF16) to outperform GPT-4.1 on deep research benchmarks. The data format... matters more than model scale.
evidence.explanation: The dev note grounds OpenResearcher's training on the Nemotron Nano 3 base checkpoint identified by the HF URL.

edges.subject: OpenResearcher/OpenResearcher-30B-A3B
edges.description: HotpotQA's 2-hop questions and annotated supporting passages were used (with MuSiQue) as the seed/golden-passage source for the corpus that backs the BM25 retriever; both questions and golden passages structurally shape the trajectories OpenResearcher trained on.

evidence.excerpt: we construct the corpus from multi-hop QA datasets: HotpotQA (2-hop questions requiring two pieces of linked evidence) and MuSiQue
evidence.explanation: Names HotpotQA as one of the multi-hop QA seed sources for the OpenResearcher-style corpus.

edges.subject: OpenResearcher/OpenResearcher-30B-A3B
edges.description: MuSiQue (loaded via bdsaglam/musique on Hugging Face per the prepare_corpus.py recipe) supplied 2-4 hop question-decomposition seeds and supporting passages used to build the OpenResearcher retrieval corpus and questions.

evidence.excerpt: MuSiQue (2-4 hop questions composed from single-hop sub-questions)... dataset = load_dataset('bdsaglam/musique', split='train')
evidence.explanation: Recipe loads the bdsaglam/musique dataset to build the OpenResearcher corpus.

edges.subject: OpenResearcher/OpenResearcher-30B-A3B

evidence.excerpt: The original OpenResearcher corpus uses 15M documents from FineWeb (https://huggingface.co/datasets/HuggingFaceFW/fineweb) as distractors alongside 10K golden passages... repo_id='HuggingFaceFW/fineweb'

edges.subject: Nemotron
edges.description: Nemotron-RL-Safety-v1 (~44.9k prompts / ~89.9k chosen/rejected preference pairs covering content safety, jailbreak robustness, over-refusal mitigation, demographic-bias mitigation, copyrighted-content leakage) is the NVIDIA Nemotron-RL safety dataset, released as part of NeMo Gym for RLHF training of the Nemotron family.

evidence.excerpt: The Nemotron-RL-Safety-v1 data is designed to provide labeled comparisons necessary to train Reward Models to distinguish between safe, helpful responses and undesired, non-compliant outputs.
evidence.explanation: Card identifies Nemotron-RL-Safety-v1 as a reward-model preference-pair dataset for RLHF, released by NVIDIA as part of the Nemotron-RL series.

evidence.excerpt: This dataset was utilized in the development of the [NVIDIA Nemotron] family of models.
evidence.explanation: Sibling Nemotron-RL card confirms NVIDIA Nemotron consumes this collection of NeMo Gym / Nemotron-RL datasets.

edges.subject: Nemotron
edges.description: Aegis Content Safety v2 (Nemotron Content Safety Dataset v2) prompts — covering self-harm, violence, criminal planning, profanity, and other harm-eliciting requests — are pooled into Nemotron-RL-Safety-v1 (only prompts are taken; responses are regenerated). Via the Nemotron-RL-Safety-v1 RLHF dataset, Aegis prompts are part of Nemotron-family RLHF training data.

evidence.excerpt: [Nemotron Content Safety Dataset v2](https://huggingface.co/datasets/nvidia/Aegis-AI-Content-Safety-Dataset-2.0): Comprehensive coverage across a variety of content safety risks like self-harm, violence, criminal planning, profanity, and other harmful or harm-eliciting user requests.
evidence.explanation: Card lists Aegis (Nemotron Content Safety v2) as one of the open-source datasets whose prompts were pooled into Nemotron-RL-Safety-v1.

edges.subject: Nemotron
edges.description: Gretel Safety Alignment v1 prompts are pooled (prompts only, responses regenerated) into Nemotron-RL-Safety-v1, providing supplemental content-safety coverage similar to Aegis. Reaches Nemotron-family RLHF training via the Nemotron-RL-Safety-v1 aggregator.

evidence.excerpt: [Gretel Safety Alignment Dataset v1](https://huggingface.co/datasets/gretelai/gretel-safety-alignment-en-v1): Supplemental content safety dataset with a similar purpose as Nemotron Content Safety Dataset.
evidence.explanation: Card lists gretelai/gretel-safety-alignment-en-v1 as one of the underlying open-source datasets pooled into Nemotron-RL-Safety-v1.

edges.subject: Nemotron

evidence.excerpt: [HarmfulTasks](https://huggingface.co/datasets/notadib/harmful-tasks): Robustness against some templated but highly effective jailbreak attacks.
evidence.explanation: Card lists notadib/harmful-tasks as one of the underlying open-source datasets pooled into Nemotron-RL-Safety-v1 for jailbreak robustness.
